full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Explaining why our accuracy numbers differ from the paper's Table 1
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: My iSVM reproduction on make_moons gives 94.17% accuracy but the paper reports ~74.7% on its synthetic Setting 1. I know the datasets are different but want to articulate the reasons clearly. What factors explain this gap?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Pointed to dataset dimensionality, sample size, nuisance covariates, and variance from 50 repeated trials as reasons the paper's numbers are lower
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified by re-reading Section 4.1: the paper uses 4 covariates (one nuisance), randomly sampled component parameters, and reports mean±std over 50 datasets — all contributing to lower/more variable accuracy

top_5_prompts.prompt: My iSVM reproduction on make_moons gives 94.17% accuracy but the paper reports ~74.7% on its synthetic Setting 1. I know the datasets are different but want to articulate the reasons clearly. What factors explain this gap?
top_5_prompts.why_important: Understanding and honestly explaining the performance gap is worth marks in Task 2.3. This prompt helped me organise the explanation around specific methodological differences rather than vague statements.

student_declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for this task.
